gates.calibration_note: the registered per-point tolerances sit inside the sampling error of the registered M_S = 4,000 estimator on a large fraction of points: (a) T gate — T_S is a binomial fraction over n_alive ~= 3,600 with SE up to sqrt(0.25/3600) ~= 0.83 pp against a 1.0 pp tolerance (~1.2 sigma at mid-range T; ~30 grid points have T_A in the mid range); (b) O95 gate — O95_S is an empirical p95 over n_dead ~= 400 with SE ~= sqrt(0.95*0.05/400)/(0.05*lambda_c) ~= 0.218/lambda_c = 10.5 h at C48 against tolerances of 7.5-15.6 h (0.7-1.5 sigma on all 18 C48 points), so P(all C48 points pass) ~= 3e-4 by construction
gates.registered: |T_S - T_A| <= 1.0 pp AND |O95_S - O95_A| <= max(4 h, 5% of O95_A), every point

model_basis.behavioral_assumption: alive-lane silences follow a two-component exponential-mixture gap model and contender checks a Poisson process — standard neutral choices; the memoryless components are what make Arm A exact; the measured feasibility of any theta is a property of THESE tails at THESE check tempos (which is exactly why gap regime and check rate are the decision axes, bracketed wide)
model_basis.most_likely_to_flip: empirically measured gap distributions — real lane activity bunches at wake boundaries and weekends harder than any exponential mixture; that flip is not a rival model to argue about — it is the named NULL-case live probe (one git-log pass per repo), which converts this question from modeling to measurement at zero tooling cost

CLAIM_STALE_HOURS_24_order_class_reading.note: read against the burst-regime columns per the registered boundary (reading, not sweep)

reporting_only.note: registered as unable to flip the decision
